headline: Will New Profit Targets and AI Strategy at Investor Day Change Citigroup's (C) Narrative?
summary: Citigroup recently ramped up activity ahead of its May 7 Investor Day, including a series of new fixed‑income offerings in multiple currencies and regions plus fresh executive appointments across infrastructure banking and the Middle East and Africa franchise. Against this backdrop, investors are watching how new profitability targets, capital actions and leadership moves might signal Citigroup’s transition from restructuring toward a more growth‑focused phase. We’ll now examine how...

headline: How The Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT) Narrative Is Shifting Around The New US$7 Target
summary: Empire State Realty Trust is back in focus as analysts recalibrate their price targets around US$7.00, giving you a fresh reference point for where expectations currently sit. Recent research commentary suggests this reset is more about keeping models aligned with updated assumptions rather than signaling a major shift in conviction. Read on to see what is driving this new US$7.00 anchor and how you can track the story as the analyst narrative evolves. Analyst Price Targets don't always...

headline: Wall Street Dares to Ask If China’s Property Turnaround Is Close
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Just months into China’s housing downturn, the nation’s second-biggest developer declared the worst is over. headline: Citi shares fall as bank reveals investor day ROTCE targets
summary: Investing.com -- Citigroup shares have dropped 3.1% in premarket trading on Thursday after the bank's investor day presentation revealed its return on tangible common equity targets.

content.title: Citi shares fall as bank reveals investor day ROTCE targets
content.summary: Investing.com -- Citigroup shares have dropped 3.1% in premarket trading on Thursday after the bank's investor day presentation revealed its return on tangible common equity targets.

headline: Citi Stock Falls. Bank Lifts Key Financial Target as Investor Day Gets Underway.
summary: Citigroup  boosted a key profitability target on Thursday, a move that was widely expected as the bank’s investor day event was getting underway at its New York headquarters.  Shares were down 3% in premarket trading, however, as investors appeared underwhelmed by the figures.  Citi said it would target return on tangible common equity, or RoTCE, between 11% and 13%, excluding the financial impact of its Banamex stake sale, for next year and 2028.

content.title: Citi Stock Falls. Bank Lifts Key Financial Target as Investor Day Gets Underway.
content.summary: Citigroup  boosted a key profitability target on Thursday, a move that was widely expected as the bank’s investor day event was getting underway at its New York headquarters.  Shares were down 3% in premarket trading, however, as investors appeared underwhelmed by the figures.  Citi said it would target return on tangible common equity, or RoTCE, between 11% and 13%, excluding the financial impact of its Banamex stake sale, for next year and 2028.

headline: Citi unveils new profit goals, buyback plan at investor day
summary: The megabank rolled out higher profitability targets on Thursday at its first investor day in four years. Its board also authorized a multiyear share repurchase program of $30 billion.

headline: Citigroup's Profitability Target Falls Short of Expectations
summary: Citigroup issued new guidance showing it would take the lender more time to catch up to Wall Street peers than some investors hoped for. Sridhar Natarajan reports on Bloomberg Television.

headline: Dive Deposits: Citi prepares for a small-t transition
summary: After a massive reorg, a tech revamp and exits from a dozen foreign markets, an updated target for return on tangible common equity may feel “underwhelming.” But Citi may be embracing incremental change.

content.title: Dive Deposits: Citi prepares for a small-t transition
content.summary: After a massive reorg, a tech revamp and exits from a dozen foreign markets, an updated target for return on tangible common equity may feel “underwhelming.” But Citi may be embracing incremental change.

thumbnail.caption: Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser leaves following a meeting with members of the Senate Banking Committee at the U.S. Capitol Visitors Center on December 11, 2025 in Washington, DC.
